source: RCA-04 / RCA-06 student-learning quiz-submit and XP-economy readiness inventory
remainingFollowUps: SLC-1-backfill: going-forward duplicate XP writes are deduped, and scripts/historical-xp-inflation-quantification.sql now provides the required read-only quantification query. Remaining work is to run it against the target DB after SLC-4/current rollout is stable, then obtain CEO/product-comms approval before any clamp.
SLC-5: client anti-cheat now always submits and the server is the single authority, but the mastery-inclusion semantics for flagged submissions remain a product/assessment decision.
SLC-8: server-only quiz-submit cutover/live deployment proof remains environment-owned; keep the route and idempotency pins until all clients are confirmed on canonical submit.

artifacts.readinessRole: Pins the SLC-1 going-forward fix: the legacy quiz-session trigger is streak-only, while atomic_quiz_profile_update remains the sole capped XP writer.
evidence: RPC remains the single award authority
FOLLOW-UP (not faked here): a live-DB behavioral assertion

artifacts.readinessRole: Provides the read-only target-DB query required before any CEO/product-comms decision to clamp or backfill historical XP inflation.
Pins the SLC-4 fallback fix so the client fallback path routes through the canonical 7-param capped ledger writer and cannot award a second 200 XP.
evidence: the pre-SLC-4 uncapped fallback would have allowed up to 400/day

artifacts.readinessRole: Pins client/server anti-cheat convergence: the client no longer hard-rejects locally and always submits to the server authority.
evidence: the whole anti-cheat region contains NO early return before submit
flagged result renders a gentle bilingual

artifacts.readinessRole: Ensures the canonical host quiz submit route supplies p_session_id to the atomic quiz writer so idempotency and daily cap accounting bind to the session.
Ensures the mobile/public v2 quiz submit route also supplies p_session_id and surfaces the authoritative server XP result.
Keeps the quiz page shuffle_map payload contract under the pre-rollout gate so server-side score re-derivation stays aligned with displayed option order.
Records which student-learning slices are landed and which residual decisions remain outside a safe repo-only patch.
